22400.history: Requisitioned by the RIN in 1940. Returned to her owner in 1946.

22401.history: Completed in mid 1904 as passenger/cargo vessel. Displacement: 266 tons. Dimensions: 45.7 x 7.9 feet. Requisitioned by the RIN on 12 September 1939. Returned to her owner in 1946.
commanders: T/Lt. Guy William Patrick O'Donoghue, RINR (5 Dec 1939 - 2 Oct 1940)

22403.history: Completed on 1 November 1918 as naval trawler TR-39, converted to cargo ship in 1932. Displacement: 362 tons. Dimentions: 44.3 x 7.2 feet. Requisitioned by the RIN on 29 October 1939. Returned to her owner in 1943.

22404.history: Completed in 1931. Requisitioned by the RIN in 1939. Returned to her owner in 1946.
commanders: A H Khan, RINVR (4 Oct 1939 - 15 Jun 1940)

22405.history: Completed in January 1918 as Royal Navy patrol frigate Kildorough, converted to cargo ship in 1932. Displacement: 895 tons. Dimentions: 55.5 x 9.1 feet. Requisitioned by the RIN on 2 October 1939. Returned to her owner in 1944.
commanders: Lt. Maurice Gilbert Collins, RIN (3 Jan 1940 - )

22406.history: Completed in October 1924 as cargo ship. Displacement: 576 tons. Dimentions: 49.0 x 8.0 feet. Requisitioned by the RIN in 1940. Transferred to the MOWT in 1943.

22407.history: Completed in 1913. Requisitioned by the RIN in 1940. Returned to her owner in 1946.

22408.history: Completed in 1920. Displacement: 423 tons. Renamed Tanjore in early 1942. Wrecked in June 1942.

22409.history: Former Flower class sloop in service with the Calcutta Port Authority. Requisitioned by the RIN on 2 September 1939. Returned to her owner in 1946.

commanders: T/Lt. Ewan McLean Reid, RINR (1 Dec 1942 - ca.mid 43)

22414.history: Completed in November 1929 as passenger/cargo vessel. Displacement: 1185 tons. Dimensions: 70.3 x 11.8 feet. Requisitioned by the RIN on 5 October 1939. Became a store carrier in 1944. Returned to her owner in 1945.

22415.history: The Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 5 was transferred to the Royal Navy on 2 March 1942. In May 1943 she was renamed HMS Blade. Returned to the Royal Netherlands Navy on 9 April 1945 but not commissioned. Scrapped at Troon, Scotland in October 1945.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Sidney Thomas Wenlock, RNR (11 Mar 1942 - 9 Apr 1945)

22416.history: The Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 7 was transferred to the Royal Navy on 1 October 1942. Decommissioned by the Royal Navy in 1944. Broken up for scrap Llanelly in 1947.
commanders: Lt. Patrick Titley, RNR (Feb 1943 - )

22417.history: The Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 8 was transferred to the Royal Navy on 1 October 1942. Decommissioned by the Royal Navy in 1944. Scrapped at Newport in August 1944.
commanders: T/Lt. Llewellyn Winston Cleverley, RNR (5 Mar 1943 - late 1943)
T/Lt. Stanley McEwen Crowther, RCNVR (late 1943 - )

22418.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
22418.history: Seized by the Royal Navy in Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French on 31 August 1940. Stranded and wrecked North of Benghazi on 27 May 1943. The wreck was destroyed on 19 June 1943.
commanders: Cdr. William Eric Banks, RN (6 Jul 1940 - 31 Aug 1940)

22419.history: Captured by the British at Plymouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy on 28 august 1940. Decommissioned on 6 December 1954 and scrapped in 1958.
commanders: Cdr. Maxwell Richmond, OBE, RN (6 Jul 1940 - 28 Aug 1940)

22420.built_by: F.&Ch de la Mediterranee (La Seyne, France)
22420.history: Captured by the British at Plymouth on 3 July 1940. Returned to mainland France by her Britsh crew on 25 August 1945. Stricken 17 February 1950.
commanders: Cdr. Claude Henry Brooks, RAN (6 Jul 1940 - 22 Sep 1940)
Lt. Charles Edward Stuart Basil St. George Beal, RN (22 Sep 1940 - 22 Oct 1940)

Lt. John Humphrey Michael Sowry, RNVR (Nov 1943 - 21 Feb 1945)
Lt. Derek George Smeeton, RN (21 Feb 1945 - mid 1945)

22421.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Lost 14 December 1940 during a storm 25 miles south-south-west off Eddystone.
commanders: Lt. Hugh Charles James McRea, RN (6 Jul 1940 - 14 Dec 1940 (+))

22422.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Returned to the Free French Navy but laid up in reserve at Hartepool, U.K in mid 1944. Returned to Cherbourg on 9 September 1945. Stricken 31 August 1950.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. John Douglas Hayes, RN (6 Jul 1940 - Apr 1941)
Lt. (retired) Francis Samuel Deveson, RNR (Apr 1941 - mid 1944)

22423.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Paid off on 9 September 1945. Returned to Cherbourgh on 23 September 1945. Stricken 17 February 1950.
commanders: Cdr. Robert William Stirling-Hamilton, RN (6 Jul 1940 - mid 1941)
Lt. Arthur John Goodeve Barff, RNR (mid 1941 - 16 Feb 1945)
T/Lt. Frank Alexander Haworth, RNVR (16 Feb 1945 - 9 Sep 1945)

22424.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy around mid 1944 but laid up in reserve at Hartelpool, U.K. Returned to Cherbourg on 9 September 1945. Stricken 31 August 1950.
commanders: Lt. Eric Lister Jones, RN (6 Jul 1940 - 1 Oct 1940)
Lt.Cdr. James Arbouin Burnett, DSC, RN (1 Oct 1940 - Jul 1941)

22425.history: Captured by the British at Portsmouth on 3 July 1940. Transferred to the Free French Navy on 31 August 1940. Laid up in reserve at Hartepool U.K. in 1943. Returned to Cherbourg on 16 September 1945. Stricken 15 May 1950.
commanders: Lt John Frederick Anthony Ashcroft, RN (6 Jul 1940 - 31 Aug 1940)

22426.history: Seized by the British on 3 July 1940 while at Portsmouth. Operated by the Royal Navy until returned to France on 6 June 1945. Stricken in September 1964.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Sidney Albert Jones, RNR (7 Jul 1940 - 21 May 1941)

22427.history: Displacement: 99 tons. Completed in June 1929. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 2 April 1940. Sold in February 1946.
commanders: T/S.Lt. John Anson Brightman, RNVR (Sep 1940 - early 41 ?)
Skr.Lt. (retired) Thomas Fraser, DSC, RD, RNR (11 Dec 1941 - 26 Sep 1943)

Lt.Cdr. (retired) Charles Everard Hughes White, DSO, RN (27 Jul 1939 - 7 Mar 1944)
A/Lt.Cdr. Alan Richardson, RNR (7 Mar 1944 - )

22429.history: Passenger / cargo vessel completed on 19 August 1920. Taken in hand for fitting out as Armed boarding vessel at Bombay on 23 January 1940. Fitting out completed on 21 February 1940. Displacement: 3129 GRT. Dimensions: 100.6 x 14 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1) Speed: 14 knots. HMS Chantala (Lt.Cdr. (retired) Christopher Ernest Inman Gibbs, RN) was mined of Tubruk, Libya on 7 December 1941.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. (retired) Christopher Ernest Inman Gibbs, RN (25 Jan 1940 - 7 Dec 1941)

22430.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
22430.history: Passenger / cargo vessel Juna completed on 16 June 1927. Taken in hand for fitting out as Armed boarding vessel at Bombay on 17 September 1939 and renamed Fiona. Fitting out completed on 24 October 1939. Displacement: 2190 GRT. Dimensions: 85.3 x 13.3 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA, 4 .303" MG AA (2x2). Speed: 11 knots. HMS Fiona (Cdr. Arthur Harold Hildreth Griffiths , RD, RNR) was bombed and sunk near Sidi Barrani, Egypt on 18 April 1941.
commanders: Lt. (emergency) Arthur Arnold Stanley Wartnaby, RN (6 Oct 1939 - Feb 1940)
Lt.Cdr. John Henry Plumer, RN (Feb 1940 - mid 40 ?)
Cdr. Arthur Harold Hildreth Griffiths, RNR (mid 40 ? - 18 Apr 1941 (+))

22431.history: Passenger vessel completed in July 1913. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1939. Displacement: 1877 GRT. Dimensions: 91.4 x 13.1 feet. Armament: 1 4" gun, 1 12 pdr AA. Speed: 21.5 knots. HMS King Orry (Cdr. Jeffery Elliot, RD, RNR) was sunk by German aircraft off Dunkirk on 30 May 1940.
commanders: Cdr. Jeffery Elliott, RD, RNR (5 Sep 1939 - 30 May 1940)

22432.history: Passenger vessel completed in June 1911. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1939. Displacement: 1783 GRT. Dimensions: 96.3 x 12.5 feet. Armament: 1 4" gun, 2 2pdr AA (2x1) Speed: 22.5 knots. Became a training vessel in 1940/1941.
commanders: Cdr. James Lunnon, RD, RNR (5 Sep 1939 - 15 May 1942)
Cdr. (retired) Henry Randolph Wilkinson, RD, RNR (15 May 1942 - Aug 1942)
Cdr. (retired) Henry Randolph Wilkinson, RD, RNR (27 Oct 1942 - late 1943)

22433.history: Passenger vessel completed in April 1905. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1939. Displacement: 1671 GRT. Dimensions: 94.9 x 12.2 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 1 12 pdr AA, 2 2pdr AA (2x1), 2 .303" MG AA (2x1). Speed: 22 knots. Became an auxiliary Anti Aircraft ship in 1941. Became an accommodation ship in 1943. Returned to her owner in 1945.
commanders: Cdr. John Charles Keith Dowding, RD, RNR (5 Sep 1939 - 9 Jan 1941)
Cdr. Arthur Ian Robertson, RNR (9 Jan 1941 - 9 Oct 1941)
Lt.Cdr. Jack Nowell Richards, RNR (9 Oct 1941 - 5 May 1942)
Cdr.(Retd.) John Hamilton Blair, DSC, RD, RNR (5 May 1942 - late 1943)

22434.built_by: William Gray & Co. (Hartepool, U.K.) : Central Marine Engineering (West Hartepool, U.K.)
22434.history: Cargo (ref.) vessel completed in August 1938. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1940. Displacement: 3122 GRT. Dimensions: 105.4 x 15.3. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1). Speed: 14.5 knots. Became a training ship in the second half of 1943. Returned to her owner in 1945.
commanders: A/Cdr. Ernest James Rigby Pollitt, RNR (17 Sep 1940 - 13 Aug 1943)
A/Cdr. (emergency) Selwyn Gerold Caygill Rawson, RN (13 Aug 1943 - early 1945)

22435.history: Passenger / cargo vessel completed on 17 December 1914. Taken in hand for fitting out as Armed boarding vessel at Bombay on 3 September 1939. Fitting out completed on 21 October 1939. Displacement: 3081 GRT. Dimensions: 100.7 x 14.1 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 1 2pdr AA, 2 .303" MG AA (2x2) Speed: 12 knots. HMS Chakla (Cdr. Leslie Charles Bach, RD, RNR) was sunk by German aircraft inside Tobruk harbour on 29 April 1941.
commanders: Cdr. Leslie Charles Bach, RD, RNR (19 Aug 1939 - 29 Apr 1941)

22436.built_by: Caledon Shipbuilding & Engineering Co. Ltd. (Dundee, Scotland)
22436.history: Passenger vessel completed in May 1917. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 1928 GRT. Dimensions: 82.4 x 11.3 feet. Armament: 1 12 pdr AA Speed: 12.5 knots. Became an auxiliary Anti Aricraft vessel in 1941.
commanders: Lt. Angus Alexander Mackenzie, RNR (9 Sep 1939 - 3 Oct 1939)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) Charles Frederick Nock, RN (3 Oct 1939 - mid 1940)
Lt. Robert Ernest Brett, RNR (mid 1940 - 8 Apr 1941)
Lt.Cdr. Joseph Stretch, RNR (8 Apr 1941 - late 1942)

22437.history: Former passenger vessel. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 2326 GRT. Dimensions: 97 x 13.5 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr. Speed: 19 knots. Became a depot ship in 1940. Returned to her owner in 1947.
commanders: Cdr. Penrose Owen Davis, RD, RNR (5 Sep 1939 - Jan 1940)
Cdr. (retired) James Christian Collie, RN (Jan 1940 - late 1940)

22438.history: Research vessel completed in November 1929. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in November 1939. Displacement: 1036 GRT. Dimensions: 67.4 x 11 feet. Armament: 1 12 pdr AA Speed: 12.5 knots. Returned to her owner in 1945.
commanders: Cdr. Penrose Owen Davis, RD, RNR (Nov 1939 - early 1942)

22439.built_by: Workman Clark & Co. Ltd. (Belfast, Northern Ireland)
22439.history: Passenger / cargo vessel completed in September 1921. Requisitioned by the Royal Navy in 1939. Displacement: 13233 GRT. Dimensions: 155.6 x 19.6 feet. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr Speed: 14 knots. HMS Vandyk (Capt. (retired) Graham Francis Winstanley Wilson, DSO, RN) was sunk by German aircraft west of Narvik, Norway on 10 June 1940.
commanders: Capt. (retired) Graham Francis Winstanley Wilson, DSO, RN (10 Nov 1939 - 10 Jun 1940)

22440.history: Passenger / cargo vessel completed on 12 December 1914. Taken in hand for fitting out as Armed boarding vessel at Singapore in January 1940. Fitting out completed on 11 March 1940. Displacement: 3033 GRT. Dimensions: 100.8 x 14.1 feet. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr, 2 2pdr AA (2x1) Speed: 12 knots. HMS Chakdina (Lt.Cdr. Walter Raleigh Hickey, RNR) was torpedoed and sunk by Italian aircraft between Tobruk and Alexandria on 5 December 1941.
commanders: R Bignall, RNR (10 Mar 1940 - Apr 1940)
Lt.Cdr. Walter Raleigh Hickey, RNR (Apr 1940 - 5 Dec 1941)

22441.built_by: D. & W. Henderson & Co. Ltd. (Glasgow, Scotland)
22441.history: Former cargo vessel completed on 28 September 1925. Displacement: 757 GRT. Dimensions: 67.1 x 10.2 feet. Armament: 1 12pdr AA. Speed: 14 knots. Became a barrage balloon vessel in 1943. HMS Fratton (Cdr. Kenelm Jocelyn Townesend Hutchings, RD, RNR) was sunk on 18 August 1944 off Normandy by a German human torpedo.
commanders: T/Lt. Alan George Roberts, RNR (24 Jul 1940 - 21 Apr 1944)
Cdr. Kenelm Jocelyn Townesend Hutchings, RD, RNR (21 Apr 1944 - 18 Aug 1944)

22442.history: Displacement: 565 tons. Completed in 1897. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in January 1940. Served as A/S (training) vessel. Returned to her owner in 1945.
commanders: Capt. (retired) Edward Carleton Stubbs, RN (20 Feb 1940 - 1 Oct 1940)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) Norman Trist Benjamin Holt, RN (1 Oct 1940 - 27 Jun 1941)
Lt.Cdr. (emergency) Lord Teynham, RN (27 Jun 1941 - 16 Oct 1941)
Lt.Cdr. (emergency) Cyril Eltringham Lowther, RAN (16 Oct 1941 - 29 Sep 1942)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) Kenneth Warden Stewart, RN (29 Sep 1942 - 5 May 1943)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) Horace Thomas Pitt, RN (5 May 1943 - 28 Feb 1945)
T/Lt. Jackson Smith, RNVR (28 Feb 1945 - mid 1945)

22443.built_by: Camper & Nicholsons Ltd. (Gosport, England, U.K.) ; Sulzer (Winterhur, Switzerland)
22443.history: Displacement: 572 tons. Completed in June 1930. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in June 1942. Served as A/S (training) vessel / tender. Returned to her owner in 1945.
commanders: A/Cdr. (retired) Arthur Reginald Willis Sayle, RD, RNR (28 Jan 1943 - Jun 1944)
Cdr. (retired) Thomas John Tressider Corfield Jenks, RN (Jun 1944 - mid 1945)

22444.history: Displacement: 356 tons. Completed in June 1899. Requisitioned by the Admiralty January 1940. Served in A/S role. Returned to her owner in 1946.
commanders: Cdr. (retired) Trevor St.Vincent Frederick Tyler, RN (29 Apr 1940 - 21 Oct 1940)
A C G Jolly, RN (21 Oct 1940 - 27 Nov 1941)
R A H Lord, RNR (27 Nov 1941 - May 1944)
T/S.Lt. James Rhodes Lloyd, KNRVR (6 Apr 1945 - 11 May 1945)
G L M Scott, RNVR (11 May 1945 - )

22445.history: Completed on 11 February 1930. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1940. Displacement: 3791 GRT Dimensions: 105.5 x 14.1 feet Armament: 6 4" AA guns (3x2), 8 2pdr AA (2x4), 10 20mm AA (10x1) Became a fighter direction ship in 1943.
commanders: A/Capt. Donald Scott McGrath, RN (24 Jun 1941 - 29 Jun 1942)
A/Capt. (retired) Charles Keith Adam, RN (29 Jun 1942 - 9 Feb 1943)
M H J Bennett, RNR (9 Feb 1943 - late 1945)

22446.built_by: William Gray & Co. (Hartepool, U.K.) : Central Marine Engineering (West Hartepool, U.K.)
22446.history: Cargo (ref.) vessel completed in September 1937. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 24 August 1940. Displacement: 3132 GRT. Dimensions: 105.4 x 15.3. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun Speed: 14.5 knots. HMS Malvernian (Cdr. John William Baillie Robertson, RNR) was bombed and heavily damaged on 1 July 1941 by German aircraft in position 47°37'N, 19°07'W, 24 dead. The ship was abandoned. She was spotted drifting on 19 July 1941 and was then finally sunk.
commanders: Cdr. John William Baillie Robertson, RNR (17 Sep 1940 - 1 Jul 1941)

22447.history: Passenger/cargo (ref.) vessel completed in May 1924. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 6 August 1940. Displacement: 6908 GRT. Dimensions: 129.6 x 16.5. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 2 2pdr AA (2x1) Speed: 14.5 knots. Returned to her owner in July 1942. Then served with Ministry of War Transport until 1946.
commanders: Capt. Newell Herbert Gale, RD, RNR (13 Aug 1940 - 25 Jan 1941)
Cdr. Charles Beresford Osborne, RD, RNR (25 Jan 1941 - mid 1942)

22448.history: Cargo vessel completed in March 1935. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1940. Displacement: 5050 GRT. Dimensions: 125.6 x 17. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun Speed: 13 knots. At 23.33 hours on 3 Feb 1941, HMS Crispin (A/Cdr Bernard Moloney, DSO, DSC, RNR) was hit in the engine room by one torpedo from the German submarine U-107 north-northwest of Rockall in position 56°38'N, 20°05'W. The ship was just detached from the dispersed convoy OB-280 together with HMS Arbutus (Lt.Cdr. H. Lloyd-Williams, RNVR), the British armed yacht HMS Philante (Capt. (retired) H.S. Bowlby, RN) and the British rescue ship Copeland (Master J. McKellar, OBE) to join the convoy SC-20 on 4 February. HMS Crispin was abandoned and foundered the following day in position 56°52'N, 20°22'W. The Commander, five officers and 14 ratings were lost. Eight survivors were picked up by the rescue ship and the remaining survivors by HMS Harvester (Lt.Cdr. M. Thornton, DSC, RN) and landed at Liverpool. Hit by U-boatSunk on 3 Feb 1941 by U-107 (Hessler).
commanders: A/Cdr. Bernard Moloney, DSO, DSC, RNR (7 Oct 1940 - 3 Feb 1941)

22449.built_by: Caledon Shipbuilding & Engineering Co. Ltd. (Dundee, Scotland)
22449.history: Passenger/cargo vessel completed in May 1930. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 7 August 1940. Displacement: 6487 GRT. Dimensions: 132 x 17.2. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1). Speed: 14.5 knots. Decommissioned on 7 April 1942 and returned to her owner. At 1555 hours on 13 November 1942, the German submarine U-81 fired two torpedoes at a convoy of four freighters and five escorts returning from Operation Torch northwest of Oran, Algeria. They heard two detonations after about three minutes and claimed one ship sunk and another damaged. The Maron (Master David Hey) was probably hit by both torpedoes and sank. The master, 67 crew members and 13 gunners were picked up by HMS Marigold (Lt. J.A.S. Halcrow, RNR) and landed at Gibraltar.
commanders: Cdr.(Retd.) John Hamilton Blair, DSC, RD, RNR (17 Sep 1940 - 7 Apr 1942)

22450.history: Cargo vessel completed in April 1940. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in August 1940. Displacement: 4890 GRT. Dimensions: 133 x 17.3. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 2 2pdr AA (2x1), 4 .303" MG AA (4x1). Speed: 14.5 knots. Returned to her owner in June 1942.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Douglas Henry Fyfe Armstrong, RNR (8 Sep 1940 - mid 1942)

22451.history: Passenger/cargo (ref.) vessel completed in June 1915. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 6 August 1940. Displacement: 6833 GRT. Dimensions: 129.9 x 16.5. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1) Speed: 14 knots. At 0240 hours on 6 May 1941 HMS Camito (Lt.Cdr. Avon Alexander Barnet, RNR) was hit aft of amidships by one torpedo from the German submarine U-97 west-southwest of Cape Clear. The U-boat had spotted the two ships at 1745 hours on 5 May and had problems to keep contact in heavy seas and bad visibility. The OBV was missed at 0202 hours with a spread of two torpedoes and three minutes later with a stern torpedo before being hit, but continued at slow speed. U-97 then chased the tanker, which caught fire after being hit by one torpedo at 0353 hours and afterwards returned to the first vessel. Heilmann thought that it is a Q-ship and left the badly damaged ship, which sank the next day in position 50°15'N, 21°16'W. Six officers and 22 ratings were lost. The survivors of both ships were picked up by HMS Orchis (Lt H. Vernon, RNR) and landed at Greenock. Hit by U-boatSunk on 6 May 1941 by U-97 (Heilmann).
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Avon Alexander Barnet, RNR (26 Sep 1940 - 6 May 1941)

22452.history: Cargo (ref.) vessel completed in January 1921. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 6833 GRT. Dimensions: 129.9 x 16.5. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1) Speed: 14 knots. At 2258 hours on 23 February 1941, U-107 fired two coups de grâce that missed because the ship suddenly continued. Also a stern torpedo fired at 2342 hours missed because it was a surface-runner. The U-boat began a long chase of the zigzagging ship and fired two torpedoes at 0758 hours on 24 February. One of them hit in the stern and caused the ship to sink in 58°55'N, 20°50'W. HMS Churchill was ordered to search for survivors, but found none. The commander, 18 officers and 122 ratings were lost.
commanders: A/Cdr. Eric Haydn Smith, RNR (7 Oct 1940 - 24 Feb 1941)

22453.built_by: Workman Clark & Co. Ltd. (Belfast, Northern Ireland)
22453.history: Cargo (ref.) vessel Erin completed in May 1932. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 5739 GRT. Dimensions: 131.5 x 16.7. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA guns (2x1) Speed: 17 knots. Fitted out as fighter catapul ship. Returned to her owner on 1 August 1942.
commanders: A/Cdr. John Owens Davies, RNR (18 Oct 1940 - 1 Aug 1942)

22454.history: Cargo (ref.) vessel completed in March 1922. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 5355 GRT. Dimensions: 121.9 x 16.5. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1) Speed: 13.5 knots. Fitted out as fighter catapul ship. HMS Patia was bombed and sunk by German He 111-bombers near 20G Buoy, Coquet Island, Northumberland, U.K.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. (retired) Edward Gavin Farr Butler, RD, RNR (23 Oct 1940 - Jan 1941)
Cdr. David Marion Burton Baker, RD, RNR (Jan 1941 - 27 Apr 1941 (+))

22455.built_by: J. Readhead & Sons Ltd. (South Shields, U.K.)
22455.history: Cargo vessel completed on 14 March 1930. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 13 September 1940. Displacement: 5886 GRT. Dimensions: 129.8 x 16.8. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun Speed: 13.5 knots. Returned to her owner in November 1941. At 0112 hours on 29 September 1942 the unescorted Registan (Master Charles Spencer Bartlett) was torpedoed and sunk by U-332 about 140 miles east of Barbados. Eleven crew members and five gunners were lost. The master, 34 crew members and three gunners were picked up on 30 September by the Argentinian merchant Rio Neuquen and landed at Pernambuco on 11 October.
commanders: A/Cdr. Eric Arthur Divers, RNR (28 Nov 1940 - mid 1941)

22456.history: Passenger / cargo (ref.) vessel completed in February 1926. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1940. Displacement: 6746 GRT. Dimensions: 129.6 x 16.5. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 2 2pdr AA (2x1), 4 .303" MG AA (4x1). Speed: 14.5 knots. Fitted out as fighter catapult ship. Used as accomodation ship after having being torpedoed and heavily damaged on 26 October 1941. Paid off and returned to her owner on 26 March 1943. Hit by U-boatDamaged on 26 Oct 1941 by U-83 (Kraus).
commanders: Cdr. (retired) Robert Abercrombie Thorburn, RD, RNR (4 Nov 1940 - early 1942)
T/Lt. Charles Robert Hodder, RNR (early 1942 - 26 Mar 1943)

22457.history: Passenger / cargo vessel completed in August 1931. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in October 1940. Displacement: 7403 GRT. Dimensions: 129.3 x 17.1. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun, 4 .303" MG AA (4x1). Speed: 14 knots. Became a headquarters ship in 1943. Returned to her owner in March 1946.
commanders: Cdr. Trevor Lewis Owen, RD, RNR (14 Oct 1940 - mid 1942)
Capt. (retired) Sir James Francis Paget, RN (1 May 1943 - 1 Mar 1945)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. E C Turner, RNR (1 Mar 1945 - mid 1945)

22458.history: Passenger / cargo (ref.) vessel completed in March 1929. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in October 1940 while in Canada. Converted to Ocean boarding vessel at Bermuda completing in April 1941. Displacement: 8194 GRT. Dimensions: 128.1 x 18.1. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun. Speed: 14 knots. HMS Lady Somers (Cdr. George Learmouth Dunbar, RD, RNR) was torpedoed and sunk in the North Atlantic by the Italian submarine Morosini on 15 July 1941.
commanders: Cdr. George Learmouth Dunbar, RD, RNR (Nov 1940 - 15 Jul 1941)

22459.built_by: Chantiers et Ateliers de Provence (Port-de-Bouc, France)
22459.history: Passenger / cargo (ref.) vessel completed in October 1938. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in May 1941. Displacement: 4504 GRT. Dimensions: 115 x 15.8. Armament: 2 6" guns (2x1), 1 12pdr AA gun. Speed: 17 knots. Became a headquarters ship in late 1942. Returned to the French on 30 November 1945.
commanders: A/Cdr. Eric Arthur Divers, RNR (24 Jun 1941 - 25 Sep 1943)
Capt. Henry Samuel Allan, RNR (25 Sep 1943 - 13 Nov 1944)
A/Capt. Harold Robson Conway, RN (13 Nov 1944 - Aug 1945)
Capt. Alaster Angus Martin, DSC, RD, RNR (2 Aug 1945 - 30 Nov 1945)

22460.history: Passenger ship completed in June 1906. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 428 GRT. Dimensions: 59.6 x 7.7. Armament: 2 2pdr AA (2x1), 8 0.5" MG AA (2x4), 4 3" rocket launhers (1x4). Speed: 15.25 knots. Harbour service as of 1943. Renamed Bunting in 1944. Returned to her owner and renamed Emperor of India in 1945.
commanders: T/Lt. Charles Clifford Matthews Pawley, RNR (9 Dec 1939 - 23 Jul 1940)
T/Lt.Cdr. Bernard Ralph Booth, RNR (23 Jul 1940 - 2 Jun 1941)
T/Lt. John Malcolm Cunningham, RNR (2 Jun 1941 - mid 1943)
T/Lt. Percy Raymund Stevens, RIN (mid 1943 - 3 Sep 1943)
Cdr. (retired) Leonard Ernest Pearson, DSC, RN (3 Sep 1943 - early 1945)

22461.history: Passenger ship completed in March 1934. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 428 GRT. Dimensions: 68.2 x 9.2. Armament: 4 2pdr AA (4x1), 6 20 mm AA (6x1), 8 0.5" MG AA (2x4), 4 3" rocket launhers (1x4). Speed: 16.5 knots. Became an accommodation ship in 1944.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. (retired) John Davis, RN (9 Dec 1939 - 8 Jul 1940)
Lt. Richard Henry Austin, RNVR (8 Jul 1940 - 7 Oct 1940)
T/Lt. John McGlashan, RNR (7 Oct 1940 - 8 Dec 1940)
T/Lt. John Harries Berry, RNR (8 Dec 1940 - 21 Apr 1941)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Harry Cecil Gaffney, RNR (21 Apr 1941 - 13 Oct 1942)
T/Lt. Francis Arthur Davies, RNR (13 Oct 1942 - mid 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Nils Anker Hoyum, RNR (mid 1944 - 24 Oct 1944)
T/Lt. Edgar Seymour Frank Elliott, RNVR (24 Oct 1944 - 12 Apr 1945)
T/Lt. Gordon Brack Row, RNVR (12 Apr 1945 - May 1945)

22462.history: Passenger ship completed in 1931. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 635 GRT. Dimensions: 76.3 x 9.2. Armament: 4 2pdr AA (4x1), 4 20 mm AA (4x1), 8 0.5" MG AA (2x4), 16 .303" MG AA (4x4). Speed: 14 knots. Paid off on 17 April 1945. Returned to her owner in May 1946.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Leonard Cooper Windsor, RN (14 Oct 1939 - Feb 1941)
T/Lt.Cdr. Arthur James Anderson, RNR (15 Apr 1941 - 13 Oct 1942)
T/Lt. John Craib Lorimer-Allen, RNZNR (13 Oct 1942 - 6 Mar 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Reginald Charles Jones, RNVR (6 Mar 1944 - late 1944)

22463.history: Passenger ship completed on 1 May 1896. Requisitioned by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 570 GRT. Dimensions: 75.9 x 9.1. Armament: 1 12pdr AA gun, 2 2pdr AA (1x2), 4 20 mm AA (4x1), 4 3" rocket launhers (1x4). Speed: 18 knots. Became an accommodation ship on 27 January 1944. Scrapped in the Netherlands in late 1946.
commanders: A O Foden, RNVR (15 May 1940 - 29 Jun 1941)
Lt. Charles Ranson Clark, RNR (29 Jun 1941 - 25 May 1942)
T/Lt. Reginald Charles Jones, RNVR (25 May 1942 - )

22464.history: Decommissioned by the Royal Netherlands Navy on 26 March 1943 and transferred to the Royal Navy. Returned to the Royal Netherlands Navy in 1946 and recommissioned under her old name.
commanders: Lt. Philip Desmond O'Driscoll, RNR (22 Mar 1943 - late 1945)

22465.history: HMS Vengeful was lent to the Greek Navy on 23 April 1945 until 1957. Scrapped at Gateshead on 22 March 1958.

22466.history: Decommissioned on 26 February 1947 Stricken on 1 September 1962 Sold to be broken up for scrap on 15 July 1974
commanders: T/Cdr. Stanley Gilbert Nichols, USN (9 May 1945 - 3 Jan 1947)

22467.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
22467.history: Completed in March 1929. Whaler of South Georgia Co. Ltd. (Chr.Salvesen & Co., Leith, U.K.), taken over by the Admiralty in March 1940. Displacement: 253 tons. Transferred to the Soviet Union in February 1942 being renamed T-103. Returned in 1947.
commanders: R N Simonsen, RNVR (11 Sep 1940 - 2 Oct 1940)
E Jansen, RNR (2 Oct 1940 - 6 Mar 1941)
W K Alexander, RNVR (6 Mar 1941 - Jul 1941)
T/S.Lt. Thomas Costley, RNVR (Jul 1941 - early 1942)

22468.history: Former USS PT 88. Was to be transferred to the Soviet Union as RPT 4 but convoys to Northern Russia was cancelled so transferred to Great Britain instead. Returned to the USN on 19 October 1945.

22469.history: Former USS PT 90. Was to be transferred to the Soviet Union as RPT 6 but convoys to Northern Russia was cancelled so transferred to Great Britain instead. Returned to the USN on 23 October 1945.
commanders: Lt. Anthony Constantine Barrington Blomfield, DSC, RN (8 Jan 1944 - 19 Nov 1944)
T/Lt. Norman Leonard Ilett, RNVR (Jan 1945 - 1 Jan 1945)
T/Lt. Albert Hazen Moore, RNVR (Jan 1945 - mid 1945)

22470.history: Former USS PT 91. Was to be transferred to the Soviet Union as RPT 7 but convoys to Northern Russia was cancelled so transferred to Great Britain instead. Returned to the USN on 8 September 1945.

22471.history: Former USS PT 92. Was to be transferred to the Soviet Union as RPT 8 but convoys to Northern Russia was cancelled so transferred to Great Britain instead. Returned to the USN on 16 October 1945.

22472.history: Former USS PT 94. Was to be transferred to the Soviet Union as RPT 10 but convoys to Northern Russia was cancelled so transferred to Great Britain instead. Returned to the USN in October 1945.

22473.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
22473.history: Completed in 1930. Whaler of South Georgia Co. Ltd. (Chr.Salvesen & Co., Leith, U.K.), taken over by the Admiralty in September 1939. Displacement: 268 tons. HMS Sarna sank after hitting a mine laid by He 111 2/KG4 in the Suez Canal.
